page_content:
{"Question": "How do I initialize an anomaly detection pipeline with my pandas DataFrame?", "Context": "I have a pandas DataFrame with time series data and I want to perform anomaly detection on it.", "Code_Example": "import pandas as pd
from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe

# Load your data
time_series_data = pd.read_csv('sensor_data.csv')

# Initialize the anomaly pipeline with your data
anomaly_pipeline = AnomalyPipe.from_dataframe(time_series_data)

# Now the pipeline is ready for anomaly detection
print("Pipeline initialized with data shape:", anomaly_pipeline.data.shape)", "Usage": "AnomalyPipe.from_dataframe(df)"}

metadata.description: Initialize an anomaly detection pipeline with a DataFrame

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I detect statistical outliers using the z-score method?", "Context": "I have sensor readings and want to identify values that are more than 3 standard deviations away from the mean.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, detect_statistical_outliers

# Create pipeline with your data
pipeline = AnomalyPipe.from_dataframe(sensor_df)

# Detect outliers using z-score method
outliers = pipeline | detect_statistical_outliers(
    columns='temperature',
    method='zscore',
    threshold=3.0
)

# Check the results
print(f"Total outliers detected: {outliers.data['temperature_outlier_zscore'].sum()}")
print(f"Outlier percentage: {(outliers.data['temperature_outlier_zscore'].sum() / len(outliers.data) * 100):.2f}%")", "Usage": "detect_statistical_outliers(columns='temperature', method='zscore', threshold=3.0)"}

metadata.description: Detect statistical outliers using z-score method

page_content:
{"Question": "How can I use the IQR method to detect outliers that's more robust to extreme values?", "Context": "My data has some extreme outliers that might skew the z-score method, so I want to use the IQR method instead.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, detect_statistical_outliers

# Detect outliers using IQR method
iqr_outliers = pipeline | detect_statistical_outliers(
    columns=['cpu_usage', 'memory_usage'],
    method='iqr',
    threshold=1.5  # Standard IQR threshold
)

# Compare multiple columns
for col in ['cpu_usage', 'memory_usage']:
    outlier_col = f"{col}_outlier_iqr"
    outlier_count = iqr_outliers.data[outlier_col].sum()
    print(f"{col}: {outlier_count} outliers detected")", "Usage": "detect_statistical_outliers(columns=['cpu_usage', 'memory_usage'], method='iqr', threshold=1.5)"}

metadata.description: Detect outliers using robust IQR method

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I detect outliers using a rolling window approach?", "Context": "I want to detect outliers based on recent data patterns rather than the entire dataset, which is useful for streaming data.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, detect_statistical_outliers

# Detect outliers using rolling window
rolling_outliers = pipeline | detect_statistical_outliers(
    columns='sensor_value',
    method='zscore',
    threshold=2.5,
    window=50,  # Use last 50 data points
    time_column='timestamp'
)

# This will detect outliers based on the local context of the last 50 points
print(f"Rolling outliers detected: {rolling_outliers.data['sensor_value_outlier_zscore'].sum()}")

# Compare with global outlier detection
global_outliers = pipeline | detect_statistical_outliers(
    columns='sensor_value',
    method='zscore',
    threshold=2.5
)
print(f"Global outliers detected: {global_outliers.data['sensor_value_outlier_zscore'].sum()}")", "Usage": "detect_statistical_outliers(columns='sensor_value', method='zscore', threshold=2.5, window=50, time_column='timestamp')"}

metadata.description: Detect outliers using rolling window approach

page_content:
{"Question": "How can I detect contextual anomalies that consider time series patterns?", "Context": "I have time series data where values might be normal in one context but anomalous in another, like high website traffic during business hours vs. midnight.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, detect_contextual_anomalies

# Detect contextual anomalies using exponential weighted moving average
contextual_anomalies = pipeline | detect_contextual_anomalies(
    columns='website_traffic',
    time_column='timestamp',
    method='residual',
    model_type='ewm',
    threshold=2.0,
    window=24  # Consider last 24 hours for hourly data
)

# Check the results
print(f"Contextual anomalies detected: {contextual_anomalies.data['website_traffic_anomaly_residual'].sum()}")

# Look at expected vs actual values for anomalies
anomalies_df = contextual_anomalies.data[contextual_anomalies.data['website_traffic_anomaly_residual']]
print(anomalies_df[['timestamp', 'website_traffic', 'website_traffic_expected', 'website_traffic_score_residual']].head())", "Usage": "detect_contextual_anomalies(columns='website_traffic', time_column='timestamp', method='residual', model_type='ewm', threshold=2.0, window=24)"}

metadata.description: Detect contextual anomalies using time series patterns

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I use SARIMAX model for contextual anomaly detection with seasonality?", "Context": "My data has strong seasonal patterns (daily, weekly) and I want to use a more sophisticated model to capture these patterns.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, detect_contextual_anomalies

# Detect anomalies using SARIMAX model with seasonality
seasonal_anomalies = pipeline | detect_contextual_anomalies(
    columns='daily_sales',
    time_column='date',
    method='prediction_interval',
    model_type='sarimax',
    threshold=2.0,
    window=60,
    seasonal_period=7,  # Weekly seasonality
    datetime_format='%Y-%m-%d'
)

# The model will account for day-of-week patterns
print(f"Seasonal anomalies detected: {seasonal_anomalies.data['daily_sales_anomaly_prediction_interval'].sum()}")

# Check anomalies by day of week
seasonal_anomalies.data['day_of_week'] = pd.to_datetime(seasonal_anomalies.data['date']).dt.day_name()
anomalies_by_day = seasonal_anomalies.data[seasonal_anomalies.data['daily_sales_anomaly_prediction_interval']]['day_of_week'].value_counts()
print("Anomalies by day of week:")
print(anomalies_by_day)", "Usage": "detect_contextual_anomalies(columns='daily_sales', time_column='date', method='prediction_interval', model_type='sarimax', threshold=2.0, window=60, seasonal_period=7)"}

metadata.description: Use SARIMAX model for seasonal anomaly detection

page_content:
{"Question": "How can I detect collective anomalies using machine learning methods?", "Context": "I have multivariate data where anomalies might not be obvious in individual features but become apparent when considering multiple features together.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, detect_collective_anomalies

# Detect collective anomalies using Isolation Forest
collective_anomalies = pipeline | detect_collective_anomalies(
    columns=['cpu_usage', 'memory_usage', 'disk_io', 'network_traffic'],
    time_column='timestamp',
    method='isolation_forest',
    window=100,
    contamination=0.05,  # Expect 5% of data to be anomalies
    n_estimators=200
)

# Check the results
print(f"Collective anomalies detected: {collective_anomalies.data['collective_anomaly_isolation_forest'].sum()}")

# Compare with Local Outlier Factor
lof_anomalies = pipeline | detect_collective_anomalies(
    columns=['cpu_usage', 'memory_usage', 'disk_io', 'network_traffic'],
    time_column='timestamp',
    method='lof',
    window=100,
    contamination=0.05
)

print(f"LOF anomalies detected: {lof_anomalies.data['collective_anomaly_lof'].sum()}")", "Usage": "detect_collective_anomalies(columns=['cpu_usage', 'memory_usage', 'disk_io', 'network_traffic'], time_column='timestamp', method='isolation_forest', window=100, contamination=0.05)"}

metadata.description: Detect collective anomalies using Isolation Forest

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I perform seasonal decomposition and detect anomalies in residuals?", "Context": "I want to separate my time series into trend, seasonal, and residual components, then detect anomalies in the residuals.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, calculate_seasonal_residuals, detect_anomalies_from_residuals

# First decompose the time series
decomposed = pipeline | calculate_seasonal_residuals(
    columns='monthly_revenue',
    mtime_column='date',
    mseasonal_period=12,  # Monthly data with yearly seasonality
    mmethod='multiplicative'
)

# Now detect anomalies in the residuals
residual_anomalies = decomposed | detect_anomalies_from_residuals(
    columns='monthly_revenue',
    method='modified_zscore',
    threshold=3.0,
    suffix='_residual',
    output_suffix='_anomaly'
)

# Check the decomposition results
print("Seasonal decomposition completed:")
print(f"Trend values: {decomposed.data['monthly_revenue_trend'].notna().sum()}")
print(f"Seasonal values: {decomposed.data['monthly_revenue_seasonal'].notna().sum()}")
print(f"Residual anomalies: {residual_anomalies.data['monthly_revenue_anomaly'].sum()}")", "Usage": "calculate_seasonal_residuals(columns='monthly_revenue', mtime_column='date', mseasonal_period=12, mmethod='multiplicative') | detect_anomalies_from_residuals(columns='monthly_revenue', method='modified_zscore', threshold=3.0)"}

metadata.description: Perform seasonal decomposition and detect residual anomalies

page_content:
{"Question": "How can I detect change points in my time series data?", "Context": "I want to identify points where the statistical properties of my time series change significantly, like when a system configuration changes.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, detect_change_points

# Detect change points using binary segmentation
change_points = pipeline | detect_change_points(
    columns='system_performance',
    time_column='timestamp',
    min_size=30,  # Minimum 30 points between change points
    method='binary_segmentation',
    penalty='bic'
)

# Check detected change points
cp_detected = change_points.data[change_points.data['system_performance_changepoint']]
print(f"Change points detected: {len(cp_detected)}")
print("Change point timestamps:")
print(cp_detected[['timestamp', 'system_performance', 'system_performance_cp_score']].head())

# Visualize the data with change points
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.plot(change_points.data['timestamp'], change_points.data['system_performance'], alpha=0.7)
cp_times = cp_detected['timestamp']
cp_values = cp_detected['system_performance']
plt.scatter(cp_times, cp_values, color='red', s=50, label='Change Points')
plt.title('System Performance with Change Points')
plt.legend()
plt.show()", "Usage": "detect_change_points(columns='system_performance', time_column='timestamp', min_size=30, method='binary_segmentation', penalty='bic')"}

metadata.description: Detect change points using binary segmentation

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I use forecasting to detect anomalies in future patterns?", "Context": "I want to forecast my time series and identify current values that deviate significantly from what the model predicts.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, forecast_and_detect_anomalies

# Detect anomalies using ARIMA forecasting
forecast_anomalies = pipeline | forecast_and_detect_anomalies(
    columns='stock_price',
    time_column='date',
    forecast_periods=30,
    model='arima',
    threshold=2.0
)

# Check forecasting results
print(f"Forecast-based anomalies: {forecast_anomalies.data['stock_price_forecast_anomaly'].sum()}")

# Get the forecast for future periods
forecasts = forecast_anomalies.forecasts['forecast_anomalies_arima']['stock_price']
print("Future forecasts:")
print(forecasts.head())

# Compare different forecasting models
exp_smooth_anomalies = pipeline | forecast_and_detect_anomalies(
    columns='stock_price',
    time_column='date',
    model='exponential_smoothing',
    seasonal_period=5,  # Weekly patterns for daily data
    threshold=2.0
)

print(f"Exponential smoothing anomalies: {exp_smooth_anomalies.data['stock_price_forecast_anomaly'].sum()}")", "Usage": "forecast_and_detect_anomalies(columns='stock_price', time_column='date', forecast_periods=30, model='arima', threshold=2.0)"}

metadata.description: Detect anomalies using time series forecasting
Combine multiple anomaly detection methods using ensemble

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I get a summary of my anomaly detection results?", "Context": "After running anomaly detection, I want to understand the overall results, including counts, percentages, and distributions.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, detect_statistical_outliers, get_anomaly_summary

# Run anomaly detection
outliers = pipeline | detect_statistical_outliers(
    columns=['temperature', 'pressure'],
    method='iqr',
    threshold=1.5
)

# Get summary of results
summary = outliers | get_anomaly_summary()

print("Anomaly Detection Summary:")
print(f"Analysis Type: {summary['analysis_type']}")
print(f"Method: {summary['method']}")
print(f"Total Data Points: {summary['total_points']}")
print(f"Total Anomalies: {summary['total_anomalies']}")
print(f"Overall Anomaly Rate: {summary['total_anomaly_percentage']:.2f}%")

print("\nAnomalies by Column:")
for col, stats in summary['anomalies_by_column'].items():
    print(f"  {col}: {stats['count']} anomalies ({stats['percentage']:.2f}%)")
    if stats['score_stats']:
        print(f"    Score range: {stats['score_stats']['min_score']:.2f} - {stats['score_stats']['max_score']:.2f}")

# Get time distribution if available
if 'time_distribution' in summary:
    print("\nTime Distribution:")
    for col, dist in summary['time_distribution'].items():
        print(f"  {col}: {dict(list(dist.items())[:5])}  # First 5 periods")", "Usage": "get_anomaly_summary()"}

metadata.description: Get comprehensive summary of anomaly detection results

page_content:
{"Question": "How can I identify the most severe anomalies in my data?", "Context": "I want to find the top anomalies by score to investigate the most significant outliers first.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, detect_contextual_anomalies, get_top_anomalies

# Run contextual anomaly detection
contextual_results = pipeline | detect_contextual_anomalies(
    columns='sensor_value',
    time_column='timestamp',
    method='residual',
    model_type='sarimax',
    threshold=2.0
)

# Get top 10 anomalies
top_anomalies = contextual_results | get_top_anomalies(
    n=10,
    time_column='timestamp'
)

print("Top 10 Anomalies:")
print(top_anomalies[['time', 'metric', 'value', 'expected', 'score']].round(3))

# Get top anomalies without time info
top_by_score = contextual_results | get_top_anomalies(n=5)

print("\nTop 5 by Score:")
for idx, row in top_by_score.iterrows():
    print(f"Score: {row['score']:.3f}, Value: {row['value']:.2f}, Expected: {row['expected']:.2f}")", "Usage": "get_top_anomalies(n=10, time_column='timestamp')"}

metadata.description: Identify the most severe anomalies by score

page_content:
{"Question": "How can I chain multiple anomaly detection analyses together?", "Context": "I want to apply multiple different anomaly detection methods in sequence and compare their results.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import (
    AnomalyPipe, 
    detect_statistical_outliers,
    detect_contextual_anomalies,
    calculate_seasonal_residuals,
    detect_anomalies_from_residuals,
    get_anomaly_summary
)

# Chain multiple analyses together
comprehensive_analysis = (
    AnomalyPipe.from_dataframe(time_series_data)
    | detect_statistical_outliers(
        columns='value',
        method='zscore',
        threshold=3.0
    )
    | detect_contextual_anomalies(
        columns='value',
        time_column='date',
        method='residual',
        model_type='ewm'
    )
    | calculate_seasonal_residuals(
        columns='value',
        mtime_column='date',
        mseasonal_period=12
    )
    | detect_anomalies_from_residuals(
        columns='value',
        method='modified_zscore'
    )
)

# Now we have results from multiple methods
print("Statistical outliers:", comprehensive_analysis.data['value_outlier_zscore'].sum())
print("Contextual anomalies:", comprehensive_analysis.data['value_anomaly_residual'].sum())
print("Residual anomalies:", comprehensive_analysis.data['value_anomaly'].sum())

# Get summary of the latest analysis
summary = comprehensive_analysis | get_anomaly_summary()
print(f"\nFinal analysis summary: {summary['total_anomalies']} anomalies detected")", "Usage": "AnomalyPipe.from_dataframe(data) | detect_statistical_outliers(...) | detect_contextual_anomalies(...) | calculate_seasonal_residuals(...) | detect_anomalies_from_residuals(...)"}

metadata.description: Chain multiple anomaly detection analyses together

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I handle grouped data for anomaly detection across different categories?", "Context": "I have data from multiple sensors or categories and want to detect anomalies within each group separately.", "Code_Example": "from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, detect_statistical_outliers, detect_contextual_anomalies

# Detect outliers separately for each sensor
grouped_outliers = pipeline | detect_statistical_outliers(
    columns='reading',
    method='iqr',
    threshold=1.5,
    group_columns=['sensor_id', 'location']  # Group by sensor and location
)

# Each sensor/location combination will have its own thresholds
print("Outliers by group:")
outlier_summary = grouped_outliers.data.groupby(['sensor_id', 'location'])['reading_outlier_iqr'].sum()
print(outlier_summary.head())

# Contextual anomalies with grouping
grouped_contextual = pipeline | detect_contextual_anomalies(
    columns='reading',
    time_column='timestamp',
    method='residual',
    model_type='ewm',
    group_columns=['sensor_id']
)

# Compare anomaly rates across groups
anomalies_by_sensor = grouped_contextual.data.groupby('sensor_id').agg({
    'reading_anomaly_residual': 'sum',
    'reading': 'count'
})
anomalies_by_sensor['anomaly_rate'] = anomalies_by_sensor['reading_anomaly_residual'] / anomalies_by_sensor['reading']
print("\nAnomaly rates by sensor:")
print(anomalies_by_sensor.sort_values('anomaly_rate', ascending=False))", "Usage": "detect_statistical_outliers(columns='reading', method='iqr', threshold=1.5, group_columns=['sensor_id', 'location'])"}

metadata.description: Handle grouped data for category-specific anomaly detection

page_content:
{"Question": "How can I create custom visualizations from anomaly detection results?", "Context": "I want to create charts and plots to visualize the detected anomalies and their patterns.", "Code_Example": "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from anomalydetection import AnomalyPipe, detect_contextual_anomalies

# Run anomaly detection
results = (
    AnomalyPipe.from_dataframe(time_series_data)
    | detect_contextual_anomalies(
        columns='temperature',
        time_column='timestamp',
        method='residual',
        model_type='ewm',
        threshold=2.0
    )
)

# Create time series plot with anomalies highlighted
plt.figure(figsize=(15, 8))

# Plot the original data
plt.subplot(2, 1, 1)
plt.plot(results.data['timestamp'], results.data['temperature'], alpha=0.7, label='Temperature')
plt.plot(results.data['timestamp'], results.data['temperature_expected'], color='green', label='Expected')

# Highlight anomalies
anomalies = results.data[results.data['temperature_anomaly_residual']]
plt.scatter(anomalies['timestamp'], anomalies['temperature'], color='red', s=30, label='Anomalies', zorder=5)

plt.title('Temperature with Detected Anomalies')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Plot anomaly scores over time
plt.subplot(2, 1, 2)
plt.plot(results.data['timestamp'], results.data['temperature_score_residual'], color='orange')
plt.axhline(y=2.0, color='red', linestyle='--', label='Threshold')
plt.fill_between(results.data['timestamp'], 0, results.data['temperature_score_residual'], 
                 where=results.data['temperature_anomaly_residual'], alpha=0.3, color='red')
plt.title('Anomaly Scores Over Time')
plt.ylabel('Anomaly Score')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('anomaly_detection_results.png', dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

# Create distribution plot of anomaly scores
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(results.data['temperature_score_residual'], bins=50, alpha=0.7)
plt.axvline(x=2.0, color='red', linestyle='--', label='Threshold')
plt.title('Distribution of Anomaly Scores')
plt.xlabel('Anomaly Score')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')
plt.legend()
plt.show()", "Usage": "plt.plot(results.data['timestamp'], results.data['temperature']); plt.scatter(anomalies['timestamp'], anomalies['temperature'], color='red', label='Anomalies')"}

metadata.description: Create custom visualizations for anomaly detection results
